requirement.original: Analyze the current sidebar collapse/expand UI implementation and propose improvements. The user says it looks ugly.
requirement.clarified: Replace Ionicons (chevron-back, menu-outline, add-outline) in sidebar header with brutalist image assets matching the existing design language. Improve expand button styling. Reorder icons so collapse is rightmost.
requirement.what: Visual redesign of sidebar collapse/expand controls and new-session button to use brutalist icon assets instead of Ionicons
requirement.why: Current UI mixes two icon systems (brutalist PNGs and Ionicons) creating visual inconsistency that the user perceives as ugly

included: Replace collapse chevron with brutalist asset in SidebarView.tsx
Replace expand hamburger with brutalist asset in SidebarNavigator.tsx
Replace add-outline with brutalist asset in SidebarView.tsx
excluded: Changing sidebar collapse/expand animation or behavior
Updating add-outline in HomeHeader.tsx or MainView.tsx
Changing logo or title area layout

success_criteria: All sidebar header icons use the same visual style (brutalist image assets, 32x32, with tintColor)
Expand button styling is cohesive with the rest of the UI
All collapse/expand/new-session behaviors remain functionally identical
Works correctly in both light and dark themes
constraints: Must use existing brutalist icon assets (no new assets)
Must maintain hitSlop and press handler behavior
Follow existing Image pattern: contentFit='contain', inline width/height, tintColor on component

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Sidebar header icons look ugly / visually inconsistent
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Two different icon systems used in the same icon row: brutalist PNG assets for inbox/settings, Ionicons for collapse/expand/new-session
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A - gradual accumulation as features were added
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Icons were added incrementally; Ionicons are the quick default while brutalist assets require choosing from the set
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Brutalist icons have thick, hand-drawn, geometric aesthetics; Ionicons are thin, uniform-weight system icons. Mixing them creates jarring visual contrast.

context.codebase_state: Sidebar collapse/expand is functional, only visual issues
context.recent_commits: N/A - not related to recent changes

SidebarView.tsx.brutalist_pattern: Image source={require('@/assets/images/brutalist/Brutalism N.png')} contentFit='contain' style={[{ width: 32, height: 32 }]} tintColor={theme.colors.header.tint}

SidebarNavigator.tsx.expand_button_style: position absolute, top:12, left:12, 32x32, borderRadius:8, bg:theme.colors.surface

development_approach.strategy: Replace all three Ionicons in sidebar collapse/expand UI with brutalist image assets. Reorder icon group. Improve expand button styling.

collapse_sidebar.note: No clean sidebar/arrow icon in brutalist set. Options: (1) Use Brutalism 15 (4-point star, outline) as abstract 'minimize', (2) Use Brutalism 22 (cubes) for 'panels', (3) Keep a simple styled approach. User should choose.

expand_sidebar.note: Could reuse the H logo as expand button for visual connection to collapsed sidebar, or use same icon as collapse for symmetry

development_approach.validation_approach: Visual inspection on web, verify both light and dark themes, verify collapse/expand/new-session functionality unchanged

research_findings.reason: Straightforward UI consistency fix using existing design system assets; no architectural decisions or novel patterns involved
